option.value: {"/home/rafran/Bureau/princept-cms":"{\"/src/contexts/AuthContext.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`createContext`: Creates a React context for authentication-related data and functionality.\\n\\n`AuthProvider`: A React component that provides the authentication context to its child components.\\n\\n`checkAuth`: A function that verifies the user\\u0027s authentication status and updates the authentication context accordingly.\\n\\n`login`: A function that handles user login and updates the authentication context.\\n\\n`logout`: A function that handles user logout and updates the authentication context.\\n\\n`changePassword`: A function that handles changing the user\\u0027s password and automatically logs the user out for security reasons.\\n\\n`updateUserInfo`: A function that updates the user\\u0027s information in the authentication context.\\n\\n`useAuth`: A custom React hook that provides access to the authentication context.\\n\\nIn summary, this file sets up a React context for managing authentication-related data and functionality, including user login, logout, password change, and user information updates.\",\"/src/index.js\":\"This file is the entry point for a React application. It sets up the root of the application and renders the `App` component within a `React.StrictMode` component. It also includes a function called `reportWebVitals` that can be used to measure the performance of the application.\\n\\n`ReactDOM.createRoot`: Creates the root of the React application and attaches it to the DOM element with the ID \\\"root\\\".\\n\\n`root.render`: Renders the `App` component within the `React.StrictMode` component, which is a tool that helps identify potential issues in an application.\\n\\n`reportWebVitals`: A function that can be used to measure the performance of the application and report the results to an analytics endpoint.\",\"/src/utils/realEmailService.js\":\"`RealEmailService`: A class that provides methods for sending emails using different email service providers, including EmailJS, Mailjet, and a custom backend API.\\n\\n`replaceTemplateVariables`: A function that replaces placeholders in an email template with the corresponding values from a variables object.\\n\\n`sendWithEmailJS`: A function that sends an email using the EmailJS service.\\n\\n`sendWithMailjetAPI`: A function that sends an email using the Mailjet API.\\n\\n`sendWithBackendAPI`: A function that sends an email using a custom backend API.\\n\\n`sendContactNotification`: A function that sends a contact notification email to the site owner.\\n\\n`sendContactConfirmation`: A function that sends a contact confirmation email to the visitor.\\n\\n`getSiteNameFromSettings`: A utility function that retrieves the site name from the site settings.\\n\\n`sendContactEmails`: A function that sends both the contact notification and confirmation emails.\\n\\n`validateEmailConfig`: A function that validates the email configuration object.\\n\\n`realEmailService`: A singleton instance of the `RealEmailService` class.\\n\\nThis file provides a service for sending emails using different email service providers, with support for customizable email templates and variables. It also includes functions for sending contact notification and confirmation emails.\",\"/api/send-email.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a Vercel function that handles sending emails using the Mailjet API. The function performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Sets the necessary CORS headers to allow cross-origin requests.\\n2. Handles the HTTP `OPTIONS` request for preflight checks.\\n3. Checks if the request method is `POST`, and returns a 405 error if it\\u0027s not.\\n4. Extracts the email configuration, email details, and email type from the request body.\\n5. Logs the email details to the console.\\n6. Prepares the payload for the Mailjet API, including the email sender, recipient, subject, text, and HTML content.\\n7. Sends the email using the Mailjet API, with the option to enable sandbox mode.\\n8. Handles any errors that occur during the email sending process and returns a JSON response with the appropriate status code and error message.\\n9. If the email is sent successfully, it returns a JSON response with the message ID and a success message.\",\"/server/models/Language.js\":\"This file defines a `Language` class that provides methods for managing languages in a database. Here are the class and function summaries:\\n\\n`Language`: A class that provides methods for interacting with the `languages` table in a database.\\n\\n`findAll()`: Retrieves all languages from the database, ordered by their sort order.\\n`findActive()`: Retrieves all active languages from the database, ordered by their sort order.\\n`findDefault()`: Retrieves the default language from the database.\\n`findById(id)`: Retrieves a language from the database by its ID.\\n`findByCode(code)`: Retrieves a language from the database by its code.\\n`create(languageData)`: Creates a new language in the database.\\n`update(id, languageData)`: Updates an existing language in the database.\\n`toggleActive(id)`: Toggles the active state of a language in the database.\\n`setAsDefault(id)`: Sets a language as the default language in the database.\\n`delete(id)`: Deletes a language from the database.\\n`reorder(languageOrder)`: Reorders the languages in the database based on the provided order.\\n`clearDefaultLanguage()`: Clears the default status of all languages in the database.\",\"/src/contexts/LanguageContextDB.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`LanguageProvider`: A React component that provides a context for managing language-related data and functionality in an application.\\n\\n`initializeLanguages`: Initializes the language data by checking the API connection, detecting the database type, and loading the languages from the API.\\n\\n`loadLanguagesFromAPI`: Loads the language data from the API and sets it in the state.\\n\\n`apiToFrontendFormat`: Converts the language data from the API format to the frontend format.\\n\\n`frontendToApiFormat`: Converts the language data from the frontend format to the API format.\\n\\n`getDefaultLanguage`: Returns the default language.\\n\\n`getActiveLanguages`: Returns the active languages.\\n\\n`getAllLanguages`: Returns all the languages.\\n\\n`toggleLanguage`: Toggles the active state of a language.\\n\\n`setDefaultLanguage`: Sets a language as the default.\\n\\n`addLanguage`: Adds a new language.\\n\\n`updateLanguage`: Updates an existing language.\\n\\n`removeLanguage`: Removes a language.\\n\\n`reorderLanguages`: Reorders the languages.\\n\\n`getCurrentAdminLanguage`: Returns the current admin language.\\n\\n`getCurrentLanguage`: Returns the current frontend language.\\n\\n`setAdminLanguage`: Sets the current admin language.\\n\\n`setCurrentLanguageById`: Sets the current frontend language.\\n\\n`createMultilingualObject`: Creates an empty multilingual object.\\n\\n`getLocalizedValue`: Retrieves the localized value from a multilingual object.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a context and utility functions for managing language-related data and functionality in a React application.\",\"/database/init/02-default-data.sql\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides default data for the Princept CMS, including:\\n\\n1. Inserting default languages with their respective IDs, names, codes, flags, and sort order.\\n2. Inserting default site settings such as the site title, description, active theme, current admin language, maintenance mode, and creation date.\\n3. Inserting default design settings for the header style, footer style, color scheme, and typography.\\n\\nThis file is used to set up the initial configuration and data for the Princept CMS, ensuring a consistent starting point for new installations.\",\"/src/components/admin/DesignSettings.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `DesignSettings` that allows users to customize the design settings of a web application, including colors, typography, layout, and animations. The component uses several custom hooks and components to manage the state and display the design settings.\\n\\n`DesignSettings`: A React component that allows users to customize the design settings of a web application.\\n\\n`ColorSection`: A section of the `DesignSettings` component that allows users to customize the primary, secondary, accent, and background colors.\\n\\n`TypographySection`: A section of the `DesignSettings` component that allows users to customize the font family and font sizes.\\n\\n`LayoutSection`: A section of the `DesignSettings` component that allows users to customize the maximum container width, spacing, and border radius.\\n\\n`AnimationSection`: A section of the `DesignSettings` component that allows users to customize the animation duration and easing.\\n\\n`handleFileImport`: A function that imports design settings from a JSON file.\\n\\n`handleExport`: A function that exports the current design settings to a JSON file.\\n\\n`handleReset`: A function that resets the design settings to their default values.\\n\\n`handleSettingChange`: A callback function that updates the design settings when a setting is changed.\",\"/src/components/MigrationManager.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`MigrationManager`: A React functional component that manages the migration of data from localStorage to a High Availability (HA) API.\\n\\n`checkStatus`: A function that checks the status of the migration and the HA API, and updates the component\\u0027s state accordingly.\\n\\n`handleMigration`: A function that initiates the migration of data from localStorage to the HA API.\\n\\n`handleCleanup`: A function that cleans up the local storage after the migration is complete.\\n\\n`handleRestore`: A function that restores the data from a backup in case of a problem.\\n\\nThis file provides a user interface for managing the migration of data from localStorage to an HA API, including checking the status, initiating the migration, cleaning up local storage, and restoring from a backup.\",\"/src/components/admin/LanguageManagement.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`LanguageManagement`: A React component that provides a user interface for managing languages, including adding, editing, deleting, and setting default languages.\\n\\n`handleAddLanguage`: Handles the addition of a new language, validating the input and using the provided context functions to add the language.\\n\\n`handleEditLanguage`: Handles the editing of an existing language, setting the `editingLanguage` state.\\n\\n`handleSaveLanguage`: Handles the saving of an edited language, updating the language using the provided context functions.\\n\\n`handleCancelEdit`: Handles the cancellation of an edit operation, resetting the `editingLanguage` state.\\n\\n`handleDeleteLanguage`: Handles the deletion of a language, opening the delete confirmation modal.\\n\\n`confirmDelete`: Handles the confirmation of a language deletion, removing the language using the provided context function.\\n\\n`cancelDelete`: Handles the cancellation of a language deletion, closing the delete confirmation modal.\\n\\n`resetForm`: Resets the `newLanguage` state and hides the add form.\\n\\nThe file also imports and uses the `DeleteConfirmationModal` component, which is likely a separate component that handles the display and functionality of the delete confirmation modal.\",\"/src/contexts/AdminThemeContextAPI.js\":\"This file is a React context provider for managing the admin theme (dark/light mode) of a web application. It handles the initialization, saving, and toggling of the admin theme, with fallback to localStorage and system preferences when the API is not available. The file also includes a hook for migrating the admin theme from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`AdminThemeContextAPI`: A React context for managing the admin theme.\\n\\n`AdminThemeProviderAPI`: A React component that provides the admin theme context to its children.\\n\\n`useAdminThemeAPI`: A custom hook that allows access to the admin theme context.\\n\\n`useMigrateAdminTheme`: A custom hook that provides a function to migrate the admin theme from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`initializeTheme`: A function that initializes the admin theme from the API or localStorage.\\n\\n`saveTheme`: A function that saves the admin theme to the API and localStorage.\\n\\n`toggleTheme`: A function that toggles the admin theme between dark and light.\\n\\n`setDark`: A function that sets the admin theme to dark.\\n\\n`setLight`: A function that sets the admin theme to light.\",\"/src/utils/dataRecovery.js\":\"`DataRecovery`: A class that provides methods for recovering lost data from the browser\\u0027s localStorage.\\n\\n`checkLocalStorage()`: Checks the contents of the localStorage and logs relevant information to the console.\\n\\n`restoreEmergencyData()`: Restores a set of emergency data to the localStorage, including a hero section and an about section.\\n\\n`searchForBackups()`: Searches the localStorage for potential backup data and logs the contents of the found keys.\\n\\n`exportAllLocalStorage()`: Exports the entire contents of the localStorage to a JSON file for analysis.\\n\\n`diagnoseData()`: A global function that creates a `DataRecovery` instance, checks the localStorage, and restores emergency data if necessary.\\n\\n`emergencyRestore()`: A global function that creates a `DataRecovery` instance and restores the emergency data.\\n\\n`searchBackups()`: A global function that creates a `DataRecovery` instance and searches for potential backup data.\\n\\n`exportLocalStorage()`: A global function that creates a `DataRecovery` instance and exports the entire contents of the localStorage.\\n\\nThis file provides a set of tools for recovering lost data from the browser\\u0027s localStorage, including diagnostic checks, emergency data restoration, backup search, and data export functionality.\",\"/src/components/sections/ServicesSection.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `ServicesSection` that renders a section of services with a title, description, and a list of service items. The component uses various hooks and utility functions to handle theming, language, and navigation. The file also defines an `iconMap` object that maps service icons to their corresponding Lucide React components.\\n\\n`iconMap`: An object that maps service icon names to their corresponding Lucide React components.\\n\\n`ServicesSection`: A React component that renders a section of services with a title, description, and a list of service items.\\n\\n`useFrontendLanguage`: A hook that provides language-related functionality, such as getting the current language and translating content.\\n\\n`useThemeStyles`: A hook that provides theme-related functionality, such as getting CSS classes and animations based on the current theme.\\n\\n`useSections`: A hook that provides information about the enabled sections in the application.\\n\\n`smartNavigate`: A utility function that navigates to a specified section or URL based on the current language.\",\"/src/components/SectionPage.js\":\"This file is a React component called `SectionPage` that renders a page for a specific section of a website. It handles the navigation, header, footer, and content of the section, as well as any themed components and language translations.\\n\\n`SectionPage`: A React component that renders a page for a specific section of a website, handling navigation, header, footer, and content.\\n\\n`scrollToSection`: A function that scrolls to a specific section of the website, either by redirecting to a new page or scrolling to an anchor on the current page.\\n\\n`navigationItems`: A function that generates an array of navigation items based on the enabled sections.\\n\\n`ThemedHeader`: A themed header component that is rendered if available, otherwise a default header is used.\\n\\n`ThemedFooter`: A themed footer component that is rendered if available, otherwise a default footer is used.\",\"/src/store/SiteSettingsStore.js\":\"This file defines a `SiteSettingsStore` class and several utility functions to manage the site settings for a web application.\\n\\n`SiteSettingsStore`: A class that manages the site settings, including loading, saving, and updating the settings, as well as providing default settings and handling migrations.\\n\\n`initializeStore`: Initializes the site settings store by loading the settings from storage and setting up the default settings.\\n\\n`initializeDefaultSettings`: Initializes the default settings for the site, including information about the site, contact details, social media, footer, SEO, and email configuration.\\n\\n`loadFromStorage`: Loads the site settings from the storage.\\n\\n`saveToStorage`: Saves the site settings to the storage.\\n\\n`subscribe`: Allows components to subscribe to changes in the site settings.\\n\\n`notify`: Notifies all subscribers of changes to the site settings.\\n\\n`getSettings`: Returns a copy of the current site settings.\\n\\n`updateSettings`: Updates the site settings and saves them to storage.\\n\\n`updateSetting`: Updates a single setting and saves it to storage.\\n\\n`migrateSocialUrls`: Migrates the legacy social media URLs to the new social media structure.\\n\\n`updateFavicon`: Updates the favicon dynamically based on the site settings.\\n\\n`updateTitle`: Updates the document title based on the site settings.\\n\\n`updateMetaDescription`: Updates the meta description based on the site settings.\\n\\n`updateMetaKeywords`: Updates the meta keywords based on the site settings.\\n\\n`applyMetaUpdates`: Applies all the meta updates (title, description, keywords, and favicon) based on the site settings.\\n\\n`resetToDefaults`: Resets the site settings to the default values.\",\"/server/server.js\":\"This file is an Express.js server that provides an API for managing languages. It sets up various middleware, routes, and error handling, and starts the server. There are no classes defined in this file, but the following functions are present:\\n\\n`startServer`: Starts the server and tests the connection to the database before doing so.\\n\\n`process.on(\\u0027SIGTERM\\u0027)`: Gracefully shuts down the server when the SIGTERM signal is received.\\n\\n`process.on(\\u0027SIGINT\\u0027)`: Gracefully shuts down the server when the SIGINT signal is received.\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up an Express.js server with security measures, rate limiting, and routes for managing language data, and includes error handling and graceful shutdown functionality.\",\"/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It exports a custom React hook called `useThemeStyles` that manages the active theme and provides various utility functions to retrieve theme-related styles, classes, inline styles, and animations.\\n\\n`useThemeStyles`: A custom React hook that manages the active theme and provides utility functions to work with the theme.\\n\\n`useState`: A React hook that manages the state of the active theme, which is stored in the browser\\u0027s local storage.\\n\\n`useEffect`: A React hook that sets up an event listener and a periodic check to detect changes in the active theme stored in the browser\\u0027s local storage.\\n\\n`getThemeStyles`: A function that retrieves the styles for a given component based on the active theme.\\n\\n`getThemeClasses`: A function that retrieves the CSS classes for a given component based on the active theme.\\n\\n`getThemeInlineStyles`: A function that retrieves the inline styles for a given component based on the active theme.\\n\\n`getThemeAnimations`: A function that retrieves the animations for the active theme.\\n\\n`getCurrentTheme`: A function that returns the currently active theme.\\n\\n`isTheme`: A function that checks if the active theme matches the provided theme ID.\\n\\n`getThemeCategory`: A function that returns the category of the active theme based on a predefined mapping.\",\"/src/components/footers/AllThemedFooters.js\":\"This file exports a set of React components that represent different footer designs for a website. Each component has a unique style and layout, catering to different types of websites and industries. The file also includes a utility function `getMultilingualText` that handles the display of multilingual text.\\n\\n`Footer`: A component that renders different footer variants based on the provided `variant` prop.\\n\\n`DefaultFooter`: A clean and professional-looking footer with company information, navigation links, and contact details.\\n\\n`GitHubFooter`: An authentic GitHub-style footer with navigation, platform, support, company, and legal sections.\\n\\n`AirbnbFooter`: A coral-themed Airbnb-style footer with a card-like layout for navigation and community information.\\n\\n`SpotifyFooter`: A dark music-themed footer with an audio visualizer effect and Spotify-inspired design.\\n\\n`SlackFooter`: A professional and colorful Slack-inspired footer with a hexagon layout.\\n\\n`NotionFooter`: A clean and minimal Notion-style footer with a block-like layout.\\n\\n`StripeFooter`: An elegant and premium Stripe-inspired footer with a wave design.\\n\\n`FigmaFooter`: A creative and colorful Figma-inspired footer with a design tool-like layout.\\n\\n`DiscordFooter`: A gaming-themed Discord-style footer with a server-like layout.\\n\\n`LinkedInFooter`: A professional and blue-themed LinkedIn-style footer.\\n\\n`ShopifyFooter`: An e-commerce-focused Shopify-inspired footer with a marketplace design.\\n\\n`NetflixFooter`: A cinematic and streaming-themed Netflix-style footer.\\n\\n`AppleFooter`: A minimalist and premium Apple-inspired footer.\\n\\n`DribbbleFooter`: A creative and portfolio-focused Dribbble-style footer.\\n\\n`YoutubeFooter`: A red and video-themed YouTube-style footer with a video player layout.\\n\\n`MediumFooter`: A clean and reading-focused Medium-style footer with an article-like layout.\\n\\n`TwitchFooter`: A purple and gaming-themed Twitch-style footer.\\n\\n`InstagramFooter`: A gradient and social-focused Instagram-style footer.\\n\\n`WhatsAppFooter`: A green and messaging-themed WhatsApp-style footer.\\n\\n`BehanceFooter`: A blue and portfolio-focused Behance-style footer.\\n\\n`UberFooter`: A black and white Uber-inspired footer with a tech-like design.\\n\\n`CursorFooter`: An AI-powered and code-themed footer with a terminal-like layout.\\n\\n`getMultilingualText`: A utility function that handles the display of multilingual text.\",\"/src/components/sections/CTASection.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines a React component called `CTASection` that renders a call-to-action section with a title, description, and a button. The component uses the `useThemeStyles` hook to apply different styles based on the current theme. The component also includes some floating elements and a background pattern to create a visually appealing design.\",\"/src/components/EmailTemplateTest.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`EmailTemplateTest`: This is a React component that provides a user interface for testing a multilingual email template system using EmailJS.\\n\\n`handleLanguageChange`: This function updates the selected language and the test data based on the selected language.\\n\\n`handleTest`: This function performs a test of the email template system by sending a contact email using the provided test data and settings.\\n\\n`validateConfiguration`: This function checks the configuration of the email settings and returns a list of errors if the configuration is incomplete.\\n\\nThe file also imports several React hooks and utility functions to manage the state, language context, and email service.\",\"/src/components/admin/AdminLayoutTest.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it exports a functional component called `AdminLayoutTest` that renders a simple React component with some text and information about the current URL. The purpose of this component is to serve as a test for the `AdminLayout.js` file, which likely has some complex imports that are causing issues.\",\"/src/store/SiteSettingsStoreAPI.js\":\"This file defines a `SiteSettingsStoreAPI` class that manages the site settings for a Princept CMS application. It handles loading, updating, and saving the site settings, as well as providing utility functions for working with the settings.\\n\\n`SiteSettingsStoreAPI`: A class that manages the site settings for a Princept CMS application, including loading, updating, and saving the settings, as well as providing utility functions for working with the settings.\\n\\n`initialize`: Initializes the site settings by loading them from an API or using default values if the API is not available.\\n\\n`loadFromAPI`: Loads the site settings from the API and converts them to the format used by the store.\\n\\n`initializeDefaultSettings`: Initializes the site settings with default values.\\n\\n`mergeWithDefaults`: Merges the current site settings with the default settings, handling any necessary migrations or conversions.\\n\\n`createMultilingualObject`: Creates a multilingual object with a default value and preserves existing values.\\n\\n`migrateContactTextFields`: Migrates the contact text fields to the multilingual format.\\n\\n`migrateSocialNetworks`: Migrates the social network settings to the correct format.\\n\\n`convertFromAPIFormat`: Converts the site settings from the API format to the format used by the store.\\n\\n`convertToAPIFormat`: Converts the site settings from the store format to the API format.\\n\\n`subscribe`: Allows components to subscribe to changes in the site settings.\\n\\n`notify`: Notifies all subscribed components of changes to the site settings.\\n\\n`getSettings`: Returns a copy of the current site settings.\\n\\n`updateSettings`: Updates the site settings and saves the changes to the API.\\n\\n`updateSettingsLocal`: Updates the site settings locally without saving to the API.\\n\\n`saveAllSettings`: Saves all the site settings to the API.\\n\\n`updateSetting`: Updates a single site setting.\\n\\n`migrateSocialUrls`: Migrates the legacy social network URLs to the new format.\\n\\n`updateFavicon`: Updates the favicon based on the site settings.\\n\\n`updateTitle`: Updates the document title based on the site settings.\\n\\n`getMultilingualValue`: Extracts the multilingual value from the site settings.\\n\\n`detectCurrentLanguage`: Detects the current language based on the URL or localStorage.\\n\\n`updateMetaDescription`: Updates the meta description based on the site settings.\\n\\n`updateMetaKeywords`: Updates the meta keywords based on the site settings.\\n\\n`applyMetaUpdates`: Applies all the meta updates to the document.\\n\\n`resetToDefaults`: Resets the site settings to the default values.\\n\\n`getStatus`: Returns the current status of the site settings store.\\n\\n`refresh`: Refreshes the site settings by re-initializing the store.\\n\\n`migrateFromLocalStorage`: Migrates the site settings from localStorage to the API.\",\"/src/components/ImageUpload.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `ImageUpload` that provides a user interface for uploading and previewing images. It supports both file upload and URL input, and includes features such as file size and type validation, drag-and-drop support, and a preview of the selected image.\\n\\n`ImageUpload`: A React component that allows users to upload or provide a URL for an image, with various options and features for handling the image upload and preview.\\n\\n`handleFileSelect`: A function that handles the selection of a file, performs validation checks, and sets the preview URL and the value of the component.\\n\\n`handleFileInputChange`: A function that is called when the file input changes, and passes the selected file to the `handleFileSelect` function.\\n\\n`handleDrop`: A function that handles the drop event when an image is dragged and dropped onto the upload area.\\n\\n`handleDragOver`: A function that sets the `dragActive` state when an image is dragged over the upload area.\\n\\n`handleDragLeave`: A function that sets the `dragActive` state when an image is dragged out of the upload area.\\n\\n`handleUrlChange`: A function that updates the `urlInput` state and the preview URL when the URL input changes.\\n\\n`handleUrlSubmit`: A function that updates the preview URL and the value of the component when the URL input is submitted.\\n\\n`clearImage`: A function that clears the image preview and the value of the component.\\n\\n`handleMethodChange`: A function that changes the input method (upload or URL) and clears the image.\",\"/src/components/admin/LoginPage.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `LoginPage` that handles the login functionality for an administrative panel. It includes the following:\\n\\n`LoginPage`: A React component that renders a login form, handles user authentication, and displays relevant information and error messages.\\n\\n`handleInputChange`: A function that updates the form data state when the user types in the username or password fields.\\n\\n`handleSubmit`: A function that is called when the user submits the login form, which attempts to log the user in and handles any errors.\\n\\n`checkDefaultPassword`: A function that checks if the admin is still using the default password and updates the state accordingly.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides the login functionality for an administrative panel, including handling user input, authentication, and displaying relevant information to the user.\",\"/src/store/SectionStore.js\":\"This file defines a `SectionStore` class and several functions within it. Here\\u0027s a summary of each:\\n\\n`SectionStore`: A class that manages the state and operations of sections in a web application, including loading, saving, and manipulating sections.\\n\\n`initializeDefaultSections`: A function that initializes the default sections if the section store is empty.\\n\\n`loadFromStorage`: A function that loads the sections from the local storage.\\n\\n`saveToStorage`: A function that saves the sections to the local storage.\\n\\n`subscribe`: A function that allows listeners to subscribe to changes in the section store.\\n\\n`notify`: A function that notifies all subscribed listeners of changes in the section store.\\n\\n`getSections`: A function that returns all the sections, sorted by their position.\\n\\n`getEnabledSections`: A function that returns all the enabled sections.\\n\\n`addSection`: A function that adds a new section to the store.\\n\\n`updateSection`: A function that updates an existing section in the store.\\n\\n`deleteSection`: A function that deletes a section from the store.\\n\\n`toggleSection`: A function that toggles the enabled state of a section.\\n\\n`reorderSections`: A function that reorders the sections based on a new order.\\n\\n`reorderPositions`: A function that reorders the positions of the sections.\\n\\n`getSectionTemplates`: A function that returns the available section templates.\",\"/production-ha.js\":\"This file is a Node.js Express application that serves as a backend API for a CMS (Content Management System) called \\\"Princept CMS\\\". It handles various operations related to languages, sections, site settings, themes, design settings, user preferences, authentication, and email sending. The application is designed to work in a high-availability (HA) environment, specifically using a MySQL database.\\n\\n`DatabaseHA`: A class that manages the connection and operations with the MySQL database, including event handling for initialization, connection, and synchronization.\\n\\n`sendWithMailjet`: A function that sends an email using the Mailjet email service.\\n\\n`sendWithSendGrid`: A function that sends an email using the SendGrid email service.\\n\\n`sendWithSMTP`: A function that sends an email using a custom SMTP server (Gmail, Outlook, or a custom configuration).\\n\\n`authenticateToken`: A middleware function that verifies the JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication token.\\n\\n`requireAdmin`: A middleware function that checks if the user has the \\\"admin\\\" role.\",\"/database/init/01-schema.sql\":\"This file appears to be a SQL script that creates a database schema for a content management system (CMS) called Princept CMS. It defines several tables and their relationships, as well as some default data. There are no classes or functions defined in this file. Instead, the file performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Creates a database called `princept_cms`.\\n2. Defines the following tables:\\n - `languages`: Stores information about the supported languages for the CMS.\\n - `site_settings`: Stores the configuration settings for the CMS.\\n - `sections`: Stores the content sections (CMS content) for the website.\\n - `custom_themes`: Stores the custom themes for the CMS.\\n - `design_settings`: Stores the design settings for the CMS.\\n - `user_preferences`: Stores the user preferences for the CMS.\\n - `users`: Stores the admin users for the CMS.\\n3. Inserts a default admin user with the username \\\"admin\\\" and password \\\"admin123\\\".\\n4. Inserts some default user preferences.\\n5. Creates several indexes to improve the performance of the database.\",\"/src/components/admin/AdminLayout.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `AdminLayout` that serves as the main layout for the admin section of a web application. It includes several sub-components and hooks that handle various functionalities, such as managing sections, themes, design settings, language management, email provider settings, and user settings. The file also includes a `DefaultPasswordAlert` component and a `ThemeToggle` component.\\n\\n`AdminLayoutContent`: Renders the main content of the admin layout, including the header, sidebar, and main content area.\\n\\n`handleAddSection`: Adds a new section to the application based on a selected template.\\n\\n`getCurrentTheme`: Retrieves the currently active theme from localStorage.\\n\\n`isActive`: Determines if the current URL path matches the target path for a sidebar item.\\n\\n`AdminLayout`: Wraps the `AdminLayoutContent` component with the `AdminThemeProviderAPI` context.\",\"/src/components/admin/PasswordChangeSuccessModal.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`PasswordChangeSuccessModal`: A React component that displays a modal with a success message, security measures, a countdown timer, and security tips after a successful password change.\\n\\n`styles`: A CSS animation that scales in the modal when it is displayed.\\n\\nThis file is a React component that displays a modal with information and actions related to a successful password change. It includes a countdown timer, security measures, and security tips to provide a comprehensive user experience.\",\"/src/hooks/useThemes.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It exports a custom React hook called `useThemes` that provides functionality for managing and customizing themes in a React application. Here are the functions in the file:\\n\\n`useThemes`: A custom React hook that provides state management and utility functions for working with themes, including predefined and custom themes, theme switching, theme creation, updating, deletion, import, export, and duplication.\\n\\n`getCurrentTheme`: Returns the current theme, either a predefined theme or a custom theme.\\n\\n`setTheme`: Sets the active theme.\\n\\n`createCustomTheme`: Creates a new custom theme based on an existing theme.\\n\\n`deleteCustomTheme`: Deletes a custom theme.\\n\\n`updateCustomTheme`: Updates a custom theme.\\n\\n`getAllThemes`: Returns an object containing all the predefined and custom themes.\\n\\n`getThemesByCategories`: Returns an object containing all the themes grouped by category.\\n\\n`importTheme`: Imports a theme from a JSON file.\\n\\n`exportTheme`: Exports a theme to a JSON file.\\n\\n`duplicateTheme`: Duplicates an existing theme.\\n\\n`resetToDefaultThemes`: Resets the custom themes and sets the active theme to the default.\\n\\n`getThemeById`: Retrieves a theme by its ID.\\n\\n`isCustomTheme`: Checks if a theme is a custom theme.\\n\\n`getThemePreview`: Returns a preview of a theme, including its ID, name, colors, and category.\",\"/src/services/emailProviders.js\":\"This file contains a single class `EmailProvidersService` that provides a service for managing email providers such as Gmail, Outlook, SendGrid, Mailgun, Amazon SES, and Mailjet. The service includes the following functions:\\n\\n`getAvailableProviders()`: Retrieves a list of available email providers with their configurations.\\n`getProvider(providerId)`: Retrieves a specific email provider by its ID.\\n`getProvidersByCategory()`: Retrieves email providers grouped by category (free, professional, enterprise, advanced).\\n`getPopularProviders()`: Retrieves the most popular email providers.\\n`validateProviderConfig(providerId, config)`: Validates the configuration of a specific email provider.\\n`generateSendConfig(providerId, userConfig)`: Generates the final configuration for sending emails using a specific email provider.\\n`testProviderConnection(providerId, config)`: Tests the connection to a specific email provider.\\n`isValidEmail(email)`: Utility function to validate email addresses.\\n`getProviderRecommendations()`: Provides recommendations for choosing the best email provider based on different use cases.\\n\\nThe file also exports a singleton instance of the `EmailProvidersService` class.\",\"/src/components/admin/AdminLayoutSimple.js\":\"This file contains a React component that implements a simple admin layout with a section editor and a section list. Here are the names of the classes and functions, along with a one-sentence summary for each:\\n\\n`SimpleSectionEditor`: A component that displays a section editor with the ability to search for and display a section based on its ID.\\n\\n`SimpleSectionList`: A component that displays a simple list of sections.\\n\\n`AdminLayoutSimple`: A component that serves as the main layout for the admin interface, including the header and the routing for the section editor and list.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a basic structure for an admin interface with a section editor and a section list, demonstrating the use of React Router and the handling of section data stored in the browser\\u0027s local storage.\",\"/src/components/admin/DefaultPasswordAlert.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`DefaultPasswordAlert`: This component displays an alert message to the user if they are still using the default password, and provides options to hide the alert, show/hide the current credentials, and navigate to the password change page.\\n\\n`handleDismiss`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"X\\\" button to dismiss the alert.\\n\\n`handleToggleCredentials`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Show/Hide\\\" button to toggle the visibility of the current credentials.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a reusable React component that displays a security alert and allows the user to manage their default password.\",\"/src/components/LanguageRouter.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`LanguageRouter`: A React component that handles language-related routing and redirects, ensuring that the correct language is set based on the URL path.\\n\\nThe file does the following:\\n\\n1. Checks the current URL path and ignores any admin routes.\\n2. Extracts the language code from the URL path and checks if it\\u0027s a valid active language.\\n3. If the URL path is the root (\\u0027/\\u0027), it redirects to the default language.\\n4. If the language code in the URL is not valid, it redirects to the default language.\\n5. If the language code in the URL is valid, it sets the current language accordingly.\\n6. Wraps the children components with an `RTLWrapper` component.\",\"/src/hooks/useSiteSettings.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It exports a custom React hook called `useSiteSettings` that provides functionality for managing and interacting with site settings in a React application.\\n\\n`useSiteSettings`: A custom React hook that provides access to site settings, allows updating and managing those settings, and includes utility functions for working with multilingual values and social network URLs.\\n\\n`getCurrentLanguage`: A function that attempts to detect the current language from the URL or falls back to French.\\n\\n`updateNestedSetting`: A function that updates a nested setting in the site settings object.\\n\\n`getVisibleSocialNetworks`: A function that returns an array of visible social network settings.\\n\\n`getSocialUrls`: A function that returns an object of social network URLs.\\n\\n`exportSettings`: A function that exports the current site settings as a JSON file.\\n\\n`importSettings`: A function that imports site settings from a JSON file.\\n\\n`getMultilingualValue`: A utility function that extracts the appropriate multilingual value based on the current language.\",\"/src/components/EmailTemplateInput.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `EmailTemplateInput` that allows users to input and manage email templates for multiple languages. The component provides a user interface with features such as language selection, input field, progress bar, and preview of the current template.\\n\\n`EmailTemplateInput`: A React component that renders an input field for email templates, with support for multiple languages and various UI elements to manage the templates.\\n\\n`handleInputChange`: A function that updates the value of the current language\\u0027s email template in the component\\u0027s state.\\n\\n`handleLanguageChange`: A function that updates the current admin language in the component\\u0027s state and closes the language dropdown.\\n\\n`hasContent`: A function that checks if the email template for a given language has any content.\\n\\n`getCompletionStatus`: A function that calculates the completion status of the email templates across all active languages.\",\"/src/components/EmailDebugPanel.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `EmailDebugPanel` that provides a debug panel for analyzing the configuration of email templates and EmailJS settings.\\n\\n`EmailDebugPanel`: A React component that displays a debug panel for analyzing the configuration of email templates and EmailJS settings.\\n\\n`analyzeTemplates`: A function that analyzes the email templates and EmailJS settings, and sets the `debugInfo` state with the analysis results.\\n\\n`previewTemplate`: A function that previews the email template for a given language.\",\"/src/components/DefaultLanguageRedirect.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`DefaultLanguageRedirect`: This function checks the default language from the LanguageContextDB and redirects the user to the corresponding language-specific route, or to the French route if the default language is not available.\\n\\nThe file also imports the `React` library and the `Navigate` component from the `react-router-dom` library, as well as the `useLanguage` hook from the `LanguageContextDB` context.\",\"/src/reportWebVitals.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`reportWebVitals`: This function is responsible for reporting web vitals, which are a set of metrics that measure the performance of a web page. It imports the necessary functions from the `web-vitals` library and calls them with the provided `onPerfEntry` function as a callback.\\n\\nThe file as a whole is responsible for setting up the reporting of web vitals for a web application, which can be useful for monitoring and improving the performance of the application.\",\"/src/components/sections/HeroSection.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `HeroSection` that renders a hero section with a title, subtitle, description, and buttons. The component uses various hooks and utility functions to handle theme-specific styles, navigation, and language translation. Here\\u0027s a breakdown of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`HeroSection`: A React component that renders a hero section with customizable content and styles based on the current theme.\\n\\n`getFinalStyles`: A function that determines the final styles for the hero section based on the current theme and settings.\\n\\n`getLayoutClasses`: A function that returns CSS classes for the hero section layout based on the current theme.\\n\\n`getContainerClasses`: A function that returns CSS classes for the hero section container based on the current theme.\\n\\n`getTextColor`: A function that returns the appropriate text color for the hero section based on the current theme.\\n\\n`smartNavigate`: A utility function that navigates to a specified section while considering the current language.\\n\\n`useFrontendLanguage`: A hook that provides language-related functionality, including translation and active language management.\\n\\n`useThemeStyles`: A hook that provides theme-specific styles and utilities.\\n\\n`useSections`: A hook that provides information about the enabled sections in the application.\",\"/src/App.js\":\"This file is the main entry point for a React application. It sets up the application\\u0027s routing, language handling, and authentication context. It also includes a utility for recovering data and a function to handle the application\\u0027s security by forcing a password change if the user\\u0027s password is the default.\\n\\n`AppContent`: A component that renders the application\\u0027s routes and handles the security check for the default password.\\n\\n`App`: The main application component that sets up the necessary providers, applies the current theme, and renders the `AppContent` component.\\n\\n`useDesignSettings`: A custom hook that provides the application\\u0027s design settings.\\n\\n`getTheme`: A function that retrieves the theme settings based on the active theme ID.\",\"/src/hooks/useFlashMessage.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`useFlashMessage`: A custom React hook that provides a set of functions to display and manage flash messages in a React application.\\n\\n`showMessage`: A function that sets a flash message with the provided text and type, and automatically hides the message after 5 seconds.\\n\\n`hideMessage`: A function that hides the current flash message.\\n\\n`showSuccess`: A function that displays a success flash message with the provided text.\\n\\n`showError`: A function that displays an error flash message with the provided text.\\n\\n`showInfo`: A function that displays an informational flash message with the provided text.\\n\\n`showWarning`: A function that displays a warning flash message with the provided text.\",\"/package.json\":\"This file appears to be a package.json file for a React-based project called \\\"princept-cms\\\". This is a common setup for a single-page application (SPA) that uses client-side routing, where the server should serve the same HTML file for all routes, and the client-side application handles the routing and rendering of the different pages.\",\"/src/components/LanguageInput.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `LanguageInput` that provides a multi-language input field with a dropdown to switch between languages, a progress bar to track the completion status, and various other features to handle language-specific content.\\n\\n`LanguageInput`: A React component that renders a multi-language input field with a dropdown to switch between languages, a progress bar to track the completion status, and various other features to handle language-specific content.\\n\\n`handleInputChange`: A function that updates the value of the current language in the input field.\\n\\n`handleLanguageChange`: A function that changes the current admin language and closes the language dropdown.\\n\\n`hasContent`: A function that checks if a language has any content.\\n\\n`getCompletionStatus`: A function that calculates the completion status of the input field across all active languages.\",\"/src/components/SectionRenderer.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes, but it does contain the following functions:\\n\\n`addImportantToCSS`: A utility function that adds the `!important` declaration to CSS properties.\\n\\n`convertMultilingualToString`: A function that converts a multilingual object into a string based on the current language.\\n\\n`SectionRenderer`: A React component that renders a section of the website based on the provided configuration, applying the appropriate theme and localization.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for rendering different sections of a website, such as the hero section, about section, services section, and more. The `handler` function checks the HTTP method of the incoming request and responds with a JSON object containing the status, message, and timestamp if the method is `GET`. If the method is not `GET`, the function sets the `Allow` header and returns a 405 Method Not Allowed response.\",\"/src/components/admin/SectionEditor.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `SectionEditor` that allows users to edit and manage different sections of a website, such as the hero section, about section, services section, contact section, gallery section, and testimonials section. The file also includes several helper components, such as `ColorPicker`, `FeatureEditor`, `ServiceEditor`, `GalleryEditor`, and `TestimonialsEditor`, which handle the specific functionality for each section.\\n\\n`ColorPicker`: A component that renders a color picker input with a preview.\\n\\n`FeatureEditor`: A component that allows the user to manage the features of the \\\"About\\\" section.\\n\\n`ServiceEditor`: A component that allows the user to manage the services of the \\\"Services\\\" section.\\n\\n`GalleryEditor`: A component that allows the user to manage the images in the \\\"Gallery\\\" section.\\n\\n`TestimonialsEditor`: A component that allows the user to manage the testimonials in the \\\"Testimonials\\\" section.\\n\\n`SectionEditor`: The main component that handles the overall management and editing of a section, including its content, layout, and styling.\",\"/src/components/sections/themed/SpotifySections.js\":\"This file contains four React components:\\n\\n`SpotifyHeroSection`: A section that displays a hero banner with a call-to-action button for Spotify.\\n\\n`SpotifyPlaylistsSection`: A section that displays a grid of Spotify playlists with a play button overlay.\\n\\n`SpotifyPlayerSection`: A section that displays a mock Spotify player with controls and a progress bar.\\n\\n`SpotifyFeaturesSection`: A section that displays three features of Spotify with icons and descriptions.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides the structure and styling for a Spotify-themed website or application.\",\"/src/styles/rtl.css\":\"This file contains CSS styles for right-to-left (RTL) language support. It includes the following styles and utility classes:\\n\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"]`: Applies RTL direction and text alignment to elements with the `dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"` attribute.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .flex`: Reverses the flex direction for navigation elements in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .space-x-*`: Adjusts the horizontal spacing between elements in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .text-left`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .text-right`: Reverses the text alignment in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .ml-auto`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .mr-auto`: Adjusts the left and right margins in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .pl-*`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .pr-*`: Adjusts the left and right padding in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .border-l`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .border-r`: Adjusts the left and right borders in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .justify-start`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .justify-end`: Reverses the flex justification in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .transform`: Applies a horizontal flip transformation to icons and directional elements.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] input`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] textarea`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] select`: Aligns form elements to the right in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] nav ul`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] nav li`: Adjusts the styles for navigation elements in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .grid`: Maintains the grid layout in LTR direction while the content is in RTL.\\n`.language-rtl`: Applies RTL direction and text alignment to elements with the `language-rtl` class.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .admin-container`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .admin-sidebar`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .admin-main`: Adjusts the layout of admin-related elements in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .tooltip`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .modal`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .modal-header`: Adjusts the styles for tooltips and modals in RTL layouts.\\n`.rtl-only`, `.ltr-only`: Utility classes to show or hide elements based on the direction.\\n`.app-content`: Applies a transition for changes in direction.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"]`: Applies a specific font family for RTL languages.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] h1`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] h2`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] h3`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] h4`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] h5`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] h6`: Applies a specific font family for headings in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .navbar`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .navbar-nav`: Adjusts the styles for navigation bars in RTL layouts.\\n`[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .icon-arrow-right`, `[dir\\u003d\\\"rtl\\\"] .icon-arrow-left`: Applies a horizontal flip transformation to arrow icons in RTL layouts.\",\"/netlify/functions/send-email.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a Netlify function that sends emails using the Mailjet API. The function handles CORS headers, checks the HTTP method, and then sends an email using the provided configuration and email details. It logs the email details and the response from Mailjet, and returns a JSON response with the success status and message ID.\",\"/src/hooks/useDesignSettings.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`useDesignSettings`: A custom React hook that manages design settings, including colors, typography, layout, and animation, and provides functions to update, reset, apply, export, and import the settings.\\n\\n`applyDesignToDocument`: A function that applies the design settings to the document\\u0027s CSS custom properties, including colors, font family, container max width, animation duration and easing, font sizes, spacing, and border radius.\\n\\nThis file is a React custom hook that provides a way to manage and apply design settings across a React application, allowing for easy customization and persistence of the design settings.\",\"/server/routes/languages.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It defines the following functions:\\n\\n`getAllLanguages`: Retrieves all languages.\\n`getActiveLanguages`: Retrieves all active languages.\\n`getDefaultLanguage`: Retrieves the default language.\\n`reorderLanguages`: Reorders the languages.\\n`getLanguageById`: Retrieves a language by its ID.\\n`createLanguage`: Creates a new language.\\n`updateLanguage`: Updates an existing language.\\n`toggleLanguage`: Toggles the active state of a language.\\n`setDefaultLanguage`: Sets a language as the default.\\n`deleteLanguage`: Deletes a language.\\n\\nThis file defines the routes for the language-related functionality in an Express.js application. It maps the various HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) to the corresponding controller functions that handle the language-related operations.\",\"/src/App.css\":\"This file appears to be a CSS stylesheet for a React application.
