The component uses the `useAuth` context to access the user\\u0027s information and update functions.\\n\\n`UserSettings`: A React component that provides a user interface for managing user profile information and changing the user\\u0027s password.\\n\\n`clearMessage`: A function that clears a message after a 5-second delay.\\n\\n`handleProfileUpdate`: A function that handles the update of the user\\u0027s profile information, including username, email, and current password.\\n\\n`handlePasswordChange`: A function that handles the change of the user\\u0027s password, including validating the current, new, and confirmation passwords.\\n\\n`handleInputChange`: A function that updates the form data state based on the input field.\\n\\n`togglePasswordVisibility`: A function that toggles the visibility of password fields.\\n\\n`MessageAlert`: A React component that displays a success or error message based on the provided type and message.\",\"/src/components/admin/ProtectedRoute.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`ProtectedRoute`: A React component that checks the user\\u0027s authentication and admin status, and either renders the protected content or redirects the user to the login page or displays an access denied message.\\n\\nThe file is responsible for implementing a protected route in a React application, ensuring that only authenticated and authorized users can access certain parts of the application.\",\"/src/config/api.js\":\"This file contains a singleton class `ApiConfig` that provides a centralized configuration for the backend API, and a set of predefined API endpoints.\\n\\n`ApiConfig`: A class that manages the configuration for the backend API, including the base URL, timeout, and headers.\\n\\n`getBaseURL()`: Retrieves the base URL for the API.\\n`getURL(endpoint)`: Constructs the full URL for a given API endpoint.\\n`getFetchConfig(options)`: Generates the configuration object for a fetch request, merging the default configuration with the provided options.\\n`getAuthFetchConfig(token, options)`: Generates the configuration object for an authenticated fetch request, adding the authorization header.\\n`setBaseURL(newBaseURL)`: Updates the base URL for the API.\\n`request(endpoint, options)`: Performs a fetch request to the specified API endpoint, handling errors and returning the response data.\\n`authRequest(endpoint, token, options)`: Performs an authenticated fetch request to the specified API endpoint.\\n\\n`API_ENDPOINTS`: An object containing predefined API endpoint paths, to avoid typos.\",\"/api/data/[key].js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It exports a default function `handler` that serves as an API endpoint for handling GET, POST, and DELETE requests for data stored in JSON files.\\n\\n`getFilePath`: This function generates the file path for a given key, sanitizing the key to ensure a valid file name.\\n\\n`handler`: This function is the main API endpoint that handles different HTTP methods (GET, POST, DELETE) for the data storage. It reads, writes, and deletes data files based on the request.\\n\\nIn summary, this file provides a simple API for storing and managing data in JSON files on the server-side.\",\"/src/components/MultilingualEmailSender.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `MultilingualEmailSender` that provides a multilingual email sending functionality. It allows users to select a language for the email, automatically detects the language, and selects the appropriate email templates based on the selected language. The component also displays the status of the email templates and provides feedback on the email sending process.\\n\\n`MultilingualEmailSender`: A React component that handles the multilingual email sending functionality.\\n\\n`getLanguageStatus`: A function that checks if the email templates for a given language are configured correctly.\\n\\n`handleSendEmail`: A function that handles the email sending process, including enriching the form data with the selected language and using the appropriate email service.\",\"/README.md\":\"This file is a documentation for the Create React App project, which is a popular tool for setting up a React development environment. It provides an overview of the available scripts and their functionalities, as well as information about code splitting, bundle size analysis, progressive web app development, advanced configuration, deployment, and troubleshooting. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather serves as a guide for developers who are getting started with Create React App.\",\"/CONFIGURATION_BACKEND.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a centralized configuration for the backend API URL and related settings. The key points are:\\n\\n1. The file introduces a centralized configuration file `src/config/api.js` that manages the backend API URL and other settings.\\n2. It describes how to update the backend API URL using environment variables or programmatic changes.\\n3. It provides a set of predefined API endpoints that can be used throughout the application.\\n4. It outlines the default configuration settings and the advantages of using this centralized approach.\\n5. It provides instructions on how to update the backend API URL and restart the React application.\",\"/src/components/LanguageSelector.js\":\"This file contains a React component and a custom hook related to language management in a web application.\\n\\n`LanguageSelector`: A React component that allows users to select the language of the application from a list of available languages.\\n\\n`useFrontendLanguage`: A custom React hook that provides a simplified interface for managing the current language in the frontend of the application, including functions to retrieve localized content.\\n\\nThe file also includes event listeners and state management to handle changes in the available languages, and it uses the `useLanguage` context to access language-related data and functions.\",\"/server/config/database.js\":\"This file defines a `Database` class that provides a unified interface for interacting with a database, with the ability to automatically fall back to SQLite if the MySQL connection fails.\\n\\n`Database`: A class that manages the connection to a database, with the ability to automatically switch between MySQL and SQLite based on availability.\\n\\n`initializeDatabase()`: A function that initializes the database connection, first trying to connect to MySQL and then falling back to SQLite if necessary.\\n\\n`initMySQL()`: A function that initializes the MySQL connection pool.\\n\\n`initSQLite()`: A function that initializes the SQLite database.\\n\\n`query()`: A function that executes a SQL query and returns the results.\\n\\n`run()`: A function that executes a SQL query for modification (for SQLite).\\n\\n`testConnection()`: A function that tests the database connection.\\n\\n`close()`: A function that closes the database connection.\",\"/src/components/footers/ThemedFooters.js\":\"This file exports three React components: `GitHubFooter`, `AirbnbFooter`, and `SpotifyFooter`, which are custom footer components for different website designs.\\n\\n`GitHubFooter`: A footer component with a logo, social media links, navigation links, and contact information.\\n\\n`AirbnbFooter`: A footer component with navigation links, contact information, and additional information links.\\n\\n`SpotifyFooter`: A footer component with a logo, navigation links, contact information, and useful links.\\n\\nThe file also imports several icons from the `lucide-react` library and uses the `useFrontendLanguage` hook from the `LanguageSelector` module.\",\"/src/utils/navigation.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`smartNavigate`: A function that handles navigation to a link, automatically managing sections with \\\"new page\\\" mode and redirecting to the appropriate URL based on the current language.\\n\\n`useSmartNavigation`: A custom hook that returns a function for using the `smartNavigate` function, with the enabled sections and current language pre-loaded.\\n\\nThis file provides a smart navigation system that automatically manages the behavior of navigation links based on the configuration of the enabled sections on the page. It handles different types of links (anchors, external links, internal links) and ensures that the navigation is consistent with the current language and section modes.\",\"/src/components/admin/ThemeToggle.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`ThemeToggle`: This function is a React component that renders a button that toggles the theme (dark or light) of the admin interface, using the `useAdminThemeAPI` hook to manage the theme state.\\n\\nThe file also imports the `Sun`, `Moon`, and `Monitor` icons from the `lucide-react` library, and the `useAdminThemeAPI` hook from the `../../contexts/AdminThemeContextAPI` module.\",\"/src/components/headers/ThemedHeaders.js\":\"This file exports three React components: `GitHubHeader`, `AirbnbHeader`, and `SpotifyHeader`. Each component represents a header for a different website or application, with different styles and functionality.\\n\\n`GitHubHeader`: A header component for a GitHub-like website, with a logo, navigation menu, search bar, and admin link.\\n\\n`AirbnbHeader`: A header component for an Airbnb-like website, with a logo, navigation menu, search button, and user menu.\\n\\n`SpotifyHeader`: A header component for a Spotify-like website, with a logo and navigation menu.\\n\\nThe file also imports several icons from the `lucide-react` library and uses the `useFrontendLanguage` hook to handle language-related functionality.\",\"/public/manifest.json\":\"This file appears to be a JSON configuration file for a React application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the metadata and configuration settings for the application. Here\\u0027s a summary of the key elements in the file:\\n\\n`\\\"short_name\\\"`: Provides a short name for the application.\\n`\\\"name\\\"`: Provides a longer, more descriptive name for the application.\\n`\\\"icons\\\"`: Defines the various icons to be used for the application, including a favicon and two larger icons for different screen sizes.\\n`\\\"start_url\\\"`: Specifies the starting URL for the application.\\n`\\\"display\\\"`: Indicates that the application should be displayed in standalone mode.\\n`\\\"theme_color\\\"`: Defines the primary color theme for the application.\\n`\\\"background_color\\\"`: Defines the background color for the application.\\n\\nThis file is typically used by a web server or a web browser to provide information about the application, such as its name, icons, and display settings, which can be used to create a more seamless and integrated user experience.\",\"/src/contexts/LanguageContextAPI.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`LanguageProvider`: A React component that provides a context for managing language-related data and functionality in a React application.\\n\\n`useLanguage`: A custom React hook that allows components to access the language-related data and functionality provided by the `LanguageProvider`.\\n\\n`initializeLanguages`: Initializes the language data by either loading it from an API or from localStorage, depending on the availability of the API.\\n\\n`loadLanguagesFromAPI`: Loads the language data from an API and sets it in the state.\\n\\n`loadLanguagesFromLocalStorage`: Loads the language data from localStorage and sets it in the state.\\n\\n`saveToLocalStorage`: Saves the language data to localStorage.\\n\\n`apiToFrontendFormat`: Converts the language data from the API format to the frontend format.\\n\\n`frontendToApiFormat`: Converts the language data from the frontend format to the API format.\\n\\n`getDefaultLanguage`: Returns the default language.\\n\\n`getActiveLanguages`: Returns the active languages.\\n\\n`getAllLanguages`: Returns all the languages.\\n\\n`toggleLanguage`: Toggles the active state of a language.\\n\\n`setDefaultLanguage`: Sets a language as the default.\\n\\n`addLanguage`: Adds a new language.\\n\\n`updateLanguage`: Updates an existing language.\\n\\n`removeLanguage`: Removes a language.\\n\\n`reorderLanguages`: Reorders the languages.\\n\\n`getCurrentAdminLanguage`: Returns the current admin language.\\n\\n`getCurrentLanguage`: Returns the current frontend language.\\n\\n`setAdminLanguage`: Sets the current admin language.\\n\\n`setCurrentLanguageById`: Sets the current frontend language.\\n\\n`createMultilingualObject`: Creates an empty multilingual object.\\n\\n`getLocalizedValue`: Retrieves the localized value from a multilingual object.\",\"/src/components/DynamicLanguageRouter.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it exports a React component called `DynamicLanguageRouter` that listens for a `languagesUpdated` event and forces a page reload when the event is triggered, ensuring that the application\\u0027s routes are updated to reflect the changes in the application\\u0027s language settings.\",\"/src/utils/emailService.js\":\"`EmailService`: A class that provides functionality for sending email notifications and confirmations, with support for multilingual templates and email providers.\\n\\n`replaceTemplateVariables`: Replaces variables in a template with their corresponding values.\\n\\n`getTemplateForLanguage`: Retrieves the appropriate email template for the specified language.\\n\\n`getEmailLanguage`: Determines the language to use for the email based on the form data and site settings.\\n\\n`sendContactNotification`: Sends a contact notification email to the site owner.\\n\\n`sendContactConfirmation`: Sends a contact confirmation email to the visitor.\\n\\n`sendWithMailjet`: Sends an email using the Mailjet email service.\\n\\n`sendWithSMTP`: Sends an email using SMTP.\\n\\n`simulateMailjetSend`: Simulates the sending of an email using Mailjet.\\n\\n`simulateSMTPSend`: Simulates the sending of an email using SMTP.\\n\\n`getSiteNameFromSettings`: Retrieves the site name in the appropriate language.\\n\\n`getLocaleForLanguage`: Retrieves the appropriate locale for the specified language.\\n\\n`sendContactEmails`: Sends both the contact notification and confirmation emails with multilingual support.\\n\\n`validateEmailConfig`: Validates the email configuration.\\n\\nThis file provides a service for sending email notifications and confirmations, with support for multilingual templates and email providers. It includes utility functions for handling template variables, language detection, and email sending simulation.\",\"/src/components/admin/DataMigration.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `DataMigration` that provides a user interface for managing data storage options, including the ability to migrate data between local storage and a JSON-based storage system, export and import data, and test the availability of the JSON API.\\n\\n`DataMigration`: A React component that allows users to manage data storage options, including migrating data between local storage and JSON-based storage, exporting and importing data, and testing the availability of the JSON API.\\n\\n`handleStorageTypeChange`: A function that handles the migration of data between local storage and JSON-based storage, updating the migration status and reloading the page to apply the changes.\\n\\n`handleExport`: A function that exports all data to a file, updating the migration status accordingly.\\n\\n`handleImport`: A function that imports data from a file, updating the migration status and reloading the page to display the new data.\\n\\n`checkJSONAvailability`: A function that checks the availability of the JSON API, updating the migration status accordingly.\",\"/src/utils/themeStyles.js\":\"This file exports several functions and does not contain any classes. The file provides a set of styles and animations for different website themes, which can be used to customize the appearance of various components such as the hero section, sections, and cards.\\n\\n`getThemeStyles`: Retrieves the theme-specific styles for different website themes, including GitHub, Airbnb, Spotify, Slack, Notion, Netflix, Apple, Discord, Figma, and Medium.\\n\\n`getThemeClasses`: Generates CSS classes based on the theme-specific styles for a given component (section or card).\\n\\n`getThemeInlineStyles`: Retrieves the inline styles specific to the theme for the hero section.\\n\\n`getThemeAnimations`: Retrieves the animation classes specific to the theme for the hero section.\",\"/src/components/admin/ThemeSelector.js\":\"This file is a React component that displays a theme selector interface. It allows users to preview and select from a collection of predefined themes, with various filtering and search options. The component also applies the selected theme to the entire application.\\n\\n`ThemePreviewCard`: A component that renders a preview card for a single theme, displaying its details and allowing the user to select it.\\n\\n`ThemeSelector`: The main component that manages the theme selection process, including filtering, searching, and applying the selected theme.\\n\\n`getCategoryIcon`: A helper function that returns the appropriate icon component for a given theme category.\\n\\n`getCategoryEmoji`: A helper function that returns the appropriate emoji for a given theme category.\\n\\n`handleThemeSelect`: A function that updates the active theme, applies the theme styles to the application, and displays a success message.\\n\\n`filteredThemes`: A function that filters the available themes based on the selected category.\",\"/src/hooks/useSections.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`useSections`: This custom React hook manages the state of sections, including their data, loading state, and initialization status, and provides functions to interact with the section store.\\n\\nThe file also imports the `useState` and `useEffect` hooks from React, as well as the `sectionStore` module, which is likely a custom store or API for managing section data.\\n\\nThe `useSections` hook initializes the sections and loading state from the `sectionStore`, subscribes to updates from the store, and updates the local state accordingly. It also provides functions to add, update, delete, toggle, and reorder sections, as well as retrieve section templates.\",\"/src/components/sections/GallerySection.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `GallerySection` that renders a section with a gallery of images, a title, description, and an optional call-to-action button. The component uses various hooks and utilities to handle theming, language, and navigation functionality.\\n\\n`GallerySection`: A React component that renders a section with a gallery of images, a title, description, and an optional call-to-action button.\\n\\n`useThemeStyles`: A hook that provides functions to get CSS classes and animations based on the current theme.\\n\\n`useSections`: A hook that provides information about the enabled sections.\\n\\n`useFrontendLanguage`: A hook that provides information about the current language and available languages.\\n\\n`smartNavigate`: A utility function that navigates to a specified link while considering the current language and enabled sections.\",\"/server/controllers/languageController.js\":\"This file contains a `LanguageController` class with several functions that handle various operations related to languages in an application.\\n\\n`LanguageController`: A class that provides methods for managing languages, including retrieving, creating, updating, and deleting languages.\\n\\n`getAllLanguages`: Retrieves all the languages.\\n`getActiveLanguages`: Retrieves the active languages.\\n`getDefaultLanguage`: Retrieves the default language.\\n`getLanguageById`: Retrieves a language by its ID.\\n`createLanguage`: Creates a new language.\\n`updateLanguage`: Updates an existing language.\\n`toggleLanguage`: Toggles the active/inactive state of a language.\\n`setDefaultLanguage`: Sets a language as the default.\\n`deleteLanguage`: Deletes a language.\\n`reorderLanguages`: Reorders the languages.\",\"/src/components/admin/AdminLanguageInput.js\":\"This file contains a single React component called `AdminLanguageInput` that provides a multi-language input field with a language selector. The component normalizes the input value to ensure that all active languages are represented, and it handles changes to the input value by updating the parent component\\u0027s state.\\n\\n`getNormalizedValue`: A function that normalizes the input value to ensure that all active languages are represented.\\n`handleChange`: A function that updates the parent component\\u0027s state with the new input value.\",\"/src/styles/admin-theme.css\":\"This file contains CSS styles for an independent administration theme. It defines CSS variables for various color schemes, as well as styles for the main container, header, sidebar, cards, inputs, buttons, and navigation elements. The file also includes specific styles for the dark mode of the administration interface.\\n\\n`:root`: Defines CSS variables for the light and dark mode color schemes.\\n\\n`.admin-dark`: Defines the color scheme for the dark mode of the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-container`: Applies the base styles for the administration container.\\n\\n`.admin-header`: Styles the header of the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-sidebar`: Styles the sidebar of the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-card`: Styles the cards used in the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-input`: Styles the input fields used in the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-button-primary`: Styles the primary buttons used in the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-button-secondary`: Styles the secondary buttons used in the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-text-muted`, `.admin-text-secondary`, `.admin-text-primary`: Styles the different text colors used in the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-section-card`: Styles the section cards used in the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-nav-item`: Styles the navigation items used in the administration interface.\\n\\n`.admin-bg-tertiary`: Styles the tertiary background color used in the administration interface.\",\"/src/components/admin/SectionList.js\":\"This file is a React component that displays a list of sections with various features for managing them, such as reordering, toggling visibility, deleting, and duplicating sections.\\n\\n`SortableItem`: A component that wraps a section card and provides the necessary functionality for sorting the sections using the `@dnd-kit/sortable` library.\\n\\n`SectionCard`: A component that renders a single section card with information about the section, including its type, title, description, and color settings, as well as various actions for managing the section.\\n\\n`DeleteConfirmationModal`: A custom modal component that displays a confirmation dialog before deleting a section.\\n\\n`SectionList`: The main component that manages the list of sections, including loading, creating, updating, and deleting sections, as well as handling the drag-and-drop functionality for reordering the sections.\",\"/src/components/admin/ForcePasswordChange.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `ForcePasswordChange` that displays a modal for users to change their password when it is detected that they are using the default password. The component uses various hooks and services to handle the password change process and display success/error messages.\\n\\n`ForcePasswordChange`: A React component that displays a modal for users to change their password when the default password is detected.\\n\\n`handleSubmit`: A function that handles the password change submission, performing validations and calling the authentication service to change the password.\\n\\n`handleSuccessComplete`: A function that handles the successful completion of the password change, logging the user out.\",\"/src/setupTests.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a setup file for the Jest-DOM library, which provides custom Jest matchers for asserting on DOM nodes. The file imports the `@testing-library/jest-dom` module, which allows the use of additional assertion methods like `toHaveTextContent()` when writing tests for React applications.\",\"/src/store/SectionStoreAPI.js\":\"This file contains a single class, `SectionStoreAPI`, which is responsible for managing the sections of a website. It handles the initialization, loading, and manipulation of sections, including creating, updating, and deleting sections, as well as reordering them. The file also includes several helper functions for converting between different data formats and managing the state of the API.\\n\\n`SectionStoreAPI`: A class that manages the sections of a website, including loading sections from an API, creating default sections, and providing methods for adding, updating, and deleting sections.\\n\\n`initialize`: Initializes the SectionStoreAPI by checking if the API is available and loading sections from the API or creating default sections if the API is not available.\\n\\n`loadFromAPI`: Loads sections from the API and converts them to the expected format.\\n\\n`initializeDefaultSections`: Creates a set of default sections and either creates them in the API or uses them locally if the API is not available.\\n\\n`convertToAPIFormat`: Converts a section from the legacy format to the API format.\\n\\n`convertFromAPIFormat`: Converts a section from the API format to the legacy format.\\n\\n`subscribe`: Allows listeners to subscribe to changes in the sections.\\n\\n`notify`: Notifies all subscribers of changes to the sections.\\n\\n`getLoadingState`: Returns the current loading state of the API.\\n\\n`getSections`: Returns the sections sorted by their position.\\n\\n`getEnabledSections`: Returns the enabled sections.\\n\\n`addSection`: Adds a new section to the API or the local data if the API is not available.\\n\\n`updateSection`: Updates an existing section in the API or the local data.\\n\\n`deleteSection`: Deletes a section from the API or the local data.\\n\\n`toggleSection`: Toggles the enabled state of a section.\\n\\n`reorderSections`: Reorders the sections based on a new order.\\n\\n`reorderPositions`: Reorders the positions of the sections.\\n\\n`getSectionTemplates`: Returns a list of predefined section templates.\\n\\n`getStatus`: Returns the current status of the API.\\n\\n`refresh`: Refreshes the API by resetting the initialization state and re-initializing.\",\"/server/DatabaseHA.js\":\"`DatabaseHA`: A class that manages a high-availability MySQL database connection, with automatic reconnection and health monitoring.\\n\\n`initialize()`: Initializes the MySQL connection and starts the health monitoring process.\\n\\n`connectMySQL()`: Connects to the MySQL database and tests the connection.\\n\\n`startHealthMonitoring()`: Starts the periodic health check for the MySQL connection.\\n\\n`healthCheck()`: Checks the status of the MySQL connection and attempts to reconnect if necessary.\\n\\n`query()`: Executes a SQL query against the MySQL database, handling errors and connection issues.\\n\\n`execute()`: Executes a SQL statement against the MySQL database, handling errors and connection issues.\\n\\n`getStatus()`: Returns the current status of the MySQL connection.\\n\\n`close()`: Closes the MySQL connection and stops the health monitoring process.\",\"/GUIDE_EMAILJS_TEMPLATE_UNIQUE.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a guide on how to use EmailJS to create a single email template with multilingual content. The guide covers the following topics:\\n\\n1. Concept: Explains the idea of using a single EmailJS template with customizable content for each language.\\n2. Advantages: Lists the benefits of this approach, such as simplicity, flexibility, cost-effectiveness, and maintainability.\\n3. Configuration: Provides step-by-step instructions on how to create the EmailJS template, use the available variables, and configure the content for each language in the admin interface.\\n4. Functionality: Describes the workflow of how the system detects the user\\u0027s language, selects the appropriate template, and sends the localized content to EmailJS.\\n5. Advanced Customization: Discusses options for further customization, such as using HTML templates, conditional variables, and styling.\\n6. Troubleshooting: Addresses potential issues and provides solutions for common problems.\\n7. Migration: Explains the process of migrating from the old system to the new multilingual approach.\\n8. Example: Provides a complete example of the EmailJS template and the corresponding admin configurations for French and English.\",\"/src/components/admin/LanguageSettings.js\":\"This file is a React component called `LanguageSettings` that provides a user interface for managing the languages of a website. It allows users to activate/deactivate languages, set a default language, and reorder the languages using drag and drop functionality.\\n\\n`LanguageSettings`: A React component that manages the language settings of a website.\\n\\n`handleToggleLanguage`: Toggles the activation state of a language, with a check to prevent deactivating the default language.\\n\\n`handleSetDefault`: Sets the specified language as the default language.\\n\\n`handleDragStart`: Handles the start of a drag event for a language item.\\n\\n`handleDragOver`: Handles the drag over event for a language item.\\n\\n`handleDrop`: Handles the drop event for a language item, reordering the languages accordingly.\",\"/src/components/admin/DeleteConfirmationModal.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`DeleteConfirmationModal`: A React component that displays a modal dialog to confirm the deletion of an item, with customizable title, subtitle, message, and button text.\\n\\nThe file provides a reusable modal component that can be used to confirm the deletion of an item in a React application, with a visually appealing and customizable design.\",\"/src/services/apiService.js\":\"This file contains a single class `ApiService` and several functions that are exported individually. The file provides a complete service API for the Princept CMS with high availability (HA) features, handling various types of data such as languages, sections, settings, themes, and user preferences.\\n\\n`ApiService`: A class that encapsulates the API interactions, providing methods to fetch data, create, update, and delete various resources in the CMS.\\n\\n`fetchWithRetry`: A helper function that wraps the fetch API with automatic retries and error handling.\\n\\n`getHealth`: Retrieves the health status of the API.\\n\\n`getMonitoring`: Retrieves the monitoring information for the API.\\n\\n`healthCheck`: A legacy method to test the connection to the API.\\n\\n`getLanguages`: Retrieves the list of languages.\\n\\n`getAllLanguages`: A legacy method to retrieve all languages.\\n\\n`getActiveLanguages`: Retrieves the list of active languages.\\n\\n`getDefaultLanguage`: Retrieves the default language.\\n\\n`createLanguage`: Creates a new language.\\n\\n`updateLanguage`: Updates an existing language.\\n\\n`toggleLanguage`: Toggles the status of a language.\\n\\n`deleteLanguage`: Deletes a language.\\n\\n`getSections`: Retrieves the list of sections based on filters.\\n\\n`getSectionById`: Retrieves a section by its ID.\\n\\n`createSection`: Creates a new section.\\n\\n`updateSection`: Updates an existing section.\\n\\n`deleteSection`: Deletes a section.\\n\\n`getSiteSettings`: Retrieves the site settings.\\n\\n`getSiteSetting`: Retrieves a specific site setting.\\n\\n`setSiteSetting`: Sets a site setting.\\n\\n`setSiteSettings`: Sets multiple site settings.\\n\\n`getThemes`: Retrieves the list of custom themes.\\n\\n`getActiveTheme`: Retrieves the active custom theme.\\n\\n`createTheme`: Creates a new custom theme.\\n\\n`activateTheme`: Activates a custom theme.\\n\\n`getDesignSettings`: Retrieves the design settings.\\n\\n`getDesignSetting`: Retrieves a specific design setting.\\n\\n`setDesignSetting`: Sets a design setting.\\n\\n`setDesignSettings`: Sets multiple design settings.\\n\\n`getUserPreferences`: Retrieves the user preferences.\\n\\n`getUserPreference`: Retrieves a specific user preference.\\n\\n`setUserPreference`: Sets a user preference.\\n\\n`setUserPreferences`: Sets multiple user preferences.\\n\\n`migrateLocalStorageData`: Migrates data from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`migrateAllLocalStorageData`: Migrates all data from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`isApiReachable`: Checks if the API is accessible.\\n\\n`getHAStatus`: Retrieves the status of the high availability (HA) system.\\n\\n`cleanupLocalStorage`: Cleans up the localStorage after a successful migration.\",\"/database/init/02-data.sql\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it appears to be a SQL script that sets up the initial data for a Princept CMS (Content Management System) database. The script performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Defines the existing languages in the system, including French, English, Spanish, Italian, German, Portuguese, Arabic, Swiss German, Japanese, and Korean.\\n2. Inserts the default site settings, such as the site title, description, default language, maintenance mode, contact email, social links, and SEO settings.\\n3. Inserts the default user preferences, including the current admin language, admin theme, active theme, auto-save, and show tips settings.\\n4. Inserts the default design settings, such as the primary and secondary colors, font family, layout mode, header height, and sidebar width.\\n\\nOverall, this script sets up the initial data and configurations for the Princept CMS system, providing a starting point for the application.\",\"/src/services/unifiedEmailService.js\":\"This file contains a single class `UnifiedEmailService` and several functions within that class. Here\\u0027s a summary of each:\\n\\n`UnifiedEmailService`: A class that provides a unified interface for sending emails through various email providers, handling the configuration and payload construction for different providers.\\n\\n`sendEmail`: Sends an email through the unified backend, handling the provider-specific configuration and payload construction.\\n`buildPayload`: Constructs the payload for sending an email based on the selected email provider.\\n`sendContactNotification`: Sends a contact notification email to the site owner using the configured email provider and templates.\\n`sendContactConfirmation`: Sends a contact confirmation email to the visitor using the configured email provider and templates.\\n`sendContactEmails`: Sends both the contact notification and confirmation emails.\\n`replaceTemplateVariables`: Replaces template variables in an email template with the provided values.\\n`getSiteNameFromSettings`: Retrieves the site name from the provided site settings.\\n`testProviderConnection`: Tests the connection to the configured email provider by sending a test email.\",\"/src/contexts/LanguageContext.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`createContext`: Creates a React context for managing language-related state and functionality.\\n\\n`LanguageProvider`: A React component that provides the language-related state and functionality to its child components.\\n\\n`useLanguage`: A custom React hook that allows components to access the language-related state and functionality provided by the `LanguageProvider`.\\n\\n`getDefaultLanguage`: Returns the default language object.\\n\\n`getActiveLanguages`: Returns an array of active language objects.\\n\\n`getAllLanguages`: Returns an array of all language objects.\\n\\n`toggleLanguage`: Toggles the active state of a language.\\n\\n`setDefaultLanguage`: Sets a language as the default.\\n\\n`reorderLanguages`: Changes the order of the languages.\\n\\n`setAdminLanguage`: Sets the current language for the admin interface.\\n\\n`setCurrentLanguageById`: Sets the current language for the frontend.\\n\\n`addLanguage`: Adds a new language.\\n\\n`updateLanguage`: Updates an existing language.\\n\\n`removeLanguage`: Removes a language.\\n\\n`getCurrentAdminLanguage`: Returns the current language for the admin interface.\\n\\n`getCurrentLanguage`: Returns the current language for the frontend.\\n\\n`createMultilingualObject`: Creates an empty multilingual object.\\n\\n`getLocalizedValue`: Retrieves the localized value from a multilingual object.\\n\\nThis file provides a comprehensive language management system for a React application, allowing for the management of multiple languages, their active states, default language, and localization of content.\",\"/src/components/sections/themed/AirbnbSections.js\":\"This file contains four React components:\\n\\n`AirbnbHeroSection`: A hero section with a search box for an Airbnb-like website.\\n\\n`AirbnbCategoriesSection`: A section displaying various categories of accommodations.\\n\\n`AirbnbListingsSection`: A section displaying a list of nearby accommodations.\\n\\n`AirbnbHostSection`: A section encouraging users to try hosting on the platform.\\n\\nThe file provides a comprehensive UI for an Airbnb-like website, including a hero section, category selection, listings, and a hosting section, all of which can be customized using a provided `theme` object.\",\"/package-lock.json\":\"This file appears to be a package.json file for a project called \\\"princept-cms\\\". It lists the dependencies and devDependencies for the project, which include various libraries and tools for web development, testing, and email functionality. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather serves as a configuration file for the project\\u0027s dependencies and settings.\",\"/src/components/sections/AboutSection.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `AboutSection` that renders a section of a website\\u0027s \\\"About\\\" page. The component uses various hooks and utility functions to handle theming, language, and navigation functionality. Here\\u0027s a breakdown of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`iconMap`: An object that maps icon names to their corresponding Lucide React components.\\n\\n`AboutSection`: A React component that renders an \\\"About\\\" section with a title, description, features, and a call-to-action button.\\n\\n`useFrontendLanguage`: A hook that provides language-related functionality, such as getting the current language and active languages.\\n\\n`smartNavigate`: A utility function that navigates to a specified URL while considering the current language and enabled sections.\\n\\n`useSections`: A hook that provides information about the enabled sections of the website.\\n\\n`useThemeStyles`: A hook that provides theme-related styles and animations.\",\"/server/sync/intelligentSync.js\":\"This file contains a single class `IntelligentSync` that provides a bidirectional synchronization system between a MySQL and SQLite database, automatically synchronizing data based on the `updated_at` timestamp.\\n\\n`IntelligentSync`: A class that synchronizes data between a MySQL and SQLite database, automatically resolving conflicts based on the latest timestamp.\\n\\n`fullIntelligentSync()`: Performs a complete intelligent synchronization, comparing timestamps and synchronizing the most recent version of the data.\\n\\n`syncTable(tableName, config)`: Synchronizes a specific table between the MySQL and SQLite databases, handling conflicts and updating the databases accordingly.\\n\\n`copyRowToMySQL(tableName, config, row)`: Copies a row of data from the SQLite database to the MySQL database.\\n\\n`copyRowToSQLite(tableName, config, row)`: Copies a row of data from the MySQL database to the SQLite database.\\n\\n`extractRowValues(tableName, row, targetDB)`: Extracts the values of a row of data based on the schema of the table and the target database (MySQL or SQLite).\\n\\n`quickSync()`: Performs a quick synchronization of recent modifications (within the last hour) between the MySQL and SQLite databases.\",\"/src/services/authService.js\":\"This file contains a single class, `AuthService`, which provides an authentication service for an admin user. Here\\u0027s a summary of the class and its functions:\\n\\n`AuthService`: Provides authentication-related functionality for an admin user, including login, logout, token verification, password change, and profile update.\\n\\n`login`: Authenticates the admin user by sending a POST request to the login endpoint and storing the returned token and user data in the local storage.\\n\\n`logout`: Logs out the admin user by sending a POST request to the logout endpoint and removing the token and user data from the local storage.\\n\\n`verifyToken`: Verifies the validity of the admin user\\u0027s token by sending a GET request to the verify token endpoint.\\n\\n`getToken`: Retrieves the admin user\\u0027s token from the local storage.\\n\\n`getCurrentUser`: Retrieves the current admin user\\u0027s data from the local storage.\\n\\n`isAuthenticated`: Checks if the admin user is currently authenticated by checking if a token is present in the local storage.\\n\\n`isAdmin`: Checks if the current user has the \\u0027admin\\u0027 role.\\n\\n`changePassword`: Updates the admin user\\u0027s password by sending a POST request to the change password endpoint.\\n\\n`updateProfile`: Updates the admin user\\u0027s profile by sending a POST request to the update profile endpoint and updating the user data in the local storage.\",\"/src/index.css\":\"This file appears to be a CSS stylesheet that defines global styles and custom components for a website. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a set of CSS variables, global styles, and custom component styles. Here\\u0027s a summary of the main elements in the file:\\n\\n`@import`: Imports various Google Fonts and other CSS files, including an admin theme and RTL (right-to-left) styles.\\n`@tailwind`: Applies Tailwind CSS utility classes.\\n`:root`: Defines a set of CSS variables for colors, typography, spacing, and other design properties.\\n`body`: Applies global styles to the body element, including font, color, and background.\\n`@layer components`: Defines a set of custom components with their own styles, such as sections, containers, titles, buttons, cards, and icons.\\n`@keyframes`: Defines various CSS animations, including fade-in, gradient, and mobile menu entrance.\\n`.glass-effect`: Applies a glassmorphism effect to an element.\\n`.btn-glow`: Applies a hover glow effect to a button.\\n`leaflet-container`, `leaflet-control-container`, `nav[class*\\u003d\\\"fixed\\\"]`: Ensures that the Leaflet map and navigation menu have the correct z-index.\\n`nav button, nav [role\\u003d\\\"button\\\"]`: Ensures that navigation menu text stays on a single line.\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up the global styles and custom components for a website, with a focus on consistent design, smooth animations, and responsive behavior.\",\"/src/components/sections/themed/GitHubSections.js\":\"This file contains three React components:\\n\\n`GitHubHeroSection`: A hero section component that displays a title, subtitle, and buttons with a carousel of features.\\n\\n`GitHubFeaturesSection`: A section that displays three features of GitHub with icons, titles, and descriptions.\\n\\n`GitHubTestimonialsSection`: A section that displays two testimonials from companies using GitHub, with the company name, quote, and author.\\n\\nThe file exports these three components, which can be used in a larger React application to showcase the features and benefits of GitHub.\",\"/MIGRATION_MYSQL.md\":\"This file is a comprehensive guide for migrating a project to MySQL using Docker. It outlines the steps involved in the migration process, including setting up MySQL and phpMyAdmin with Docker, testing the connection, migrating the frontend, configuring the environment, and managing the database. The file also covers the new features and capabilities introduced in the migration, such as automatic fallback to localStorage, bidirectional sync, and improved performance and error handling. Additionally, it provides instructions for testing the migration and troubleshooting common issues. The file concludes by outlining the next steps for the project, including migrating other data, enhancing the admin interface, and preparing for e-commerce functionality.\",\"/src/utils/migrationManager.js\":\"This file is a migration manager that handles the migration of data from localStorage to an API. It provides functionality to check the migration status, create a backup, migrate various data types (languages, sections, site settings, custom themes, design settings, and user preferences), mark the migration as completed, clean up localStorage, and restore from a backup.\\n\\n`MigrationManager`: A class that manages the migration of data from localStorage to an API.\\n\\n`isMigrationCompleted`: Checks if the migration has already been completed.\\n\\n`markMigrationCompleted`: Marks the migration as completed.\\n\\n`createBackup`: Creates a backup of the data in localStorage.\\n\\n`migrateSiteLanguages`: Migrates the site languages from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`migrateSections`: Migrates the sections from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`migrateSiteSettings`: Migrates the site settings from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`migrateCustomThemes`: Migrates the custom themes from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`migrateDesignSettings`: Migrates the design settings from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`migrateUserPreferences`: Migrates the user preferences from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`migrateAll`: Performs the complete migration from localStorage to the API.\\n\\n`cleanupLocalStorage`: Removes the migrated data from localStorage.\\n\\n`restoreFromBackup`: Restores the data from the backup in localStorage.\\n\\n`getStatus`: Retrieves the status of the migration.\",\"/src/utils/dataStorage.js\":\"This file contains a `DataStorage` class that provides a system for managing data with support for JSON and localStorage. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`DataStorage`: A class that manages data storage, providing methods to load, save, and migrate data between localStorage and a JSON API.\\n\\n`initializeStorage()`: Initializes the data storage, checking the availability of the JSON API and migrating data from localStorage to JSON if necessary.\\n\\n`setStorageType(type)`: Sets the type of data storage to use, either localStorage or JSON.\\n\\n`load(key)`: Loads data from the specified storage type (localStorage or JSON) using the given key.\\n\\n`save(key, data)`: Saves data to the specified storage type (localStorage or JSON) using the given key and data.\\n\\n`loadFromLocalStorage(key)`: Loads data from localStorage using the given key.\\n\\n`saveToLocalStorage(key, data)`: Saves data to localStorage using the given key and data.\\n\\n`loadFromJSON(key)`: Loads data from a JSON API using the given key.\\n\\n`saveToJSON(key, data)`: Saves data to a JSON API using the given key and data.\\n\\n`migrateToJSON()`: Migrates data from localStorage to the JSON API.\\n\\n`migrateToLocalStorage()`: Migrates data from the JSON API to localStorage.\\n\\n`exportAllData()`: Exports all data from localStorage to a downloadable JSON file.\\n\\n`importFromFile(file)`: Imports data from a JSON file.\\n\\n`checkJSONAvailability()`: Checks the availability of the JSON API.\\n\\n`autoMigrateFromLocalStorage()`: Automatically migrates data from localStorage to the JSON API.\",\"/public/index.html\":\"This file is a standard HTML template for a React application. It does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it sets up the basic structure and metadata for a React application, including:\\n\\n- Defining the HTML document structure with `\\u003chtml\\u003e`, `\\u003chead\\u003e`, and `\\u003cbody\\u003e` tags.\\n- Specifying the character encoding, viewport, and theme color for the application.\\n- Providing metadata about the application, such as the description and icons for mobile devices.\\n- Linking to the `manifest.json` file, which provides additional metadata for the application.\\n- Rendering the React application\\u0027s content within a `\\u003cdiv id\\u003d\\\"root\\\"\\u003e\\u003c/div\\u003e` element.\\n- Displaying a message for users who have JavaScript disabled.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to serve as the entry point for the React application, setting up the necessary HTML structure and metadata before the actual React code is loaded and executed.\",\"/src/components/admin/SiteSettings.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `SiteSettings` that allows the user to configure various settings for a website, including general information, branding, contact details, email templates, social media links, SEO, and advanced options. The file also includes several helper components and functions to manage the state and behavior of the `SiteSettings` component.\\n\\n`ColorPicker`: A component that renders a color picker input with a button to toggle the picker.\\n\\n`SiteSettings`: The main component that renders the site settings interface and handles the logic for updating and saving the settings.\\n\\n`getStringValue`: A utility function that converts a multilingual object value to a string.\\n\\n`handleFileImport`: A function that handles the import of site settings from a JSON file.\\n\\n`handleSave`: A function that saves all the site settings and applies any necessary updates.\\n\\n`handleReset`: A function that resets all the site settings to their default values.\",\"/src/components/AppRoutes.js\":\"This file defines the main routing structure for a React application. It uses the `react-router-dom` library to handle the routing and navigation of the application.\\n\\n`AppRoutes`: This component manages all the routes of the application, including the frontend pages, the admin pages, and the language-specific routes.\\n\\n`DefaultLanguageRedirect`: This function redirects the user to the default language page when the root URL is accessed.\\n\\n`LanguageRouter`: This component wraps the frontend pages and section pages to provide language-specific functionality.\\n\\n`ProtectedRoute`: This component ensures that the admin pages are only accessible to authorized users.\\n\\nThe file also imports and uses several other components, such as `Frontend`, `SectionPage`, `AdminLayout`, and `LoginPage`, to handle the different parts of the application.\",\"/server/config/sqlite.js\":\"This file defines a `SQLiteDatabase` class that provides a wrapper around the `sqlite3` library to interact with a SQLite database. The class handles the initialization of the database, creation of tables, and execution of SQL queries.\\n\\n`SQLiteDatabase`: A class that provides a wrapper around the `sqlite3` library to interact with a SQLite database.\\n\\n`constructor()`: Initializes the SQLite database connection and creates the necessary tables if they don\\u0027t already exist.\\n\\n`initialize()`: Initializes the database tables and inserts default data if the tables are empty.\\n\\n`query(sql, params)`: Executes a SQL query and returns the result as a Promise.\\n\\n`run(sql, params)`: Executes a SQL query that modifies the database (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) and returns the result as a Promise.\\n\\n`testConnection()`: Tests the connection to the SQLite database and returns a boolean value.\\n\\n`close()`: Closes the connection to the SQLite database and returns a Promise.\",\"/src/components/Frontend.js\":\"This file is a React component called `Frontend` that renders the main content of a website. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`Frontend`: This is the main React component that renders the header, main content, map section, and footer of the website.\\n\\n`scrollToSection`: This function scrolls the page to a specific section based on the section type and ID.\\n\\n`navigationItems`: This is an array of navigation items that are used to render the desktop and mobile navigation menus.\\n\\n`ThemedHeader` and `ThemedFooter`: These are optional themed components that can be used to render the header and footer of the website, respectively.\\n\\nThe file also includes several hooks and utility functions to manage the site\\u0027s sections, settings, and theming.\",\"/src/components/admin/SectionEditorSimple.js\":\"This file is a React component called `SectionEditorSimple` that allows users to edit and preview sections of a website. It uses various hooks and components to manage the state, update the sections, and display the section preview.\\n\\n`ColorPicker`: A component that renders a color picker input with a button to toggle the color picker.\\n\\n`SectionEditorSimple`: The main component that renders the section editor interface, including the header, editor form, sidebar, and preview.\\n\\n`getSectionTypeName`: A function that returns the human-readable name of a section type.\\n\\n`handleChange`: A function that updates the state of the section when a field is changed.\\n\\n`handleSave`: A function that saves the changes to the section and updates the state accordingly.\\n\\n`handleKeyDown`: A function that listens for the Ctrl+S keyboard shortcut and calls the `handleSave` function.\",\"/src/components/sections/TestimonialsSection.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `TestimonialsSection` that renders a section with testimonials. It handles the display of testimonials, including the ability to navigate to the contact section, and provides various styling options based on the selected theme.\\n\\n`TestimonialsSection`: A React component that renders a section with testimonials, including the ability to navigate to the contact section and various styling options based on the selected theme.\\n\\n`scrollToContact`: A function that handles the navigation to the contact section, either by scrolling to the contact section or redirecting to the contact page, depending on the navigation mode of the contact section.\\n\\n`renderStars`: A function that renders a set of star icons based on the provided rating.\",\"/src/data/themedComponents.js\":\"This file appears to be a configuration file for themed components in a web application. It defines various themed components for different brands and platforms, including headers, sections, and footers. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`themedComponents`: An object that defines the configuration for themed components for different brands and platforms, including headers, sections, and footers.\\n\\n`getThemedComponents`: A function that retrieves the themed components for a given theme ID.\\n\\n`getThemeSections`: A function that retrieves all the sections available for a given theme ID.\\n\\n`hasThemedComponents`: A function that checks if a given theme ID has any themed components (headers, sections, or footers).\\n\\nOverall, this file serves as a central repository for managing the themed components of a web application, allowing for easy configuration and customization of the user interface for different brands and platforms.\",\"/src/utils/languageDetection.js\":\"This file contains utility functions for detecting and managing language in emails. It does not contain any classes, but the following functions are defined:\\n\\n`detectCurrentLanguage`: Detects the current language based on the URL, localStorage, browser settings, or falls back to French.\\n\\n`enrichFormDataWithLanguage`: Enriches form data with the detected language and other metadata.\\n\\n`isLanguageSupported`: Validates if a given language is supported.\\n\\n`getFallbackLanguage`: Obtains the most appropriate fallback language based on the supported languages and a default language.\\n\\n`useLanguageDetection`: Creates a React hook to use language detection in components, providing functions to get the current language, enrich form data, and check language support.\",\"/src/components/MapSection.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`loadLeaflet`: Loads the Leaflet library and its CSS by dynamically adding the necessary script and link tags to the document.\\n\\n`initMap`: Initializes the Leaflet map, sets its center and zoom level, adds the OpenStreetMap tiles, and adds a marker with a popup displaying the provided title, description, and address.\\n\\n`MapSection`: A React functional component that renders a section with a map, a title, and a description. It uses the `loadLeaflet` and `initMap` functions to set up the map.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a reusable React component that displays a map with a marker and additional information, using the Leaflet library to handle the map functionality.\",\"/GUIDE_UTILISATION_TEMPLATE_EXISTANT.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a guide that explains how to use an existing EmailJS template with multilingual content configured in the admin interface. The guide covers the following:\\n\\n1. The EmailJS template and its main variables.\\n2. The new features in the admin interface, including multilingual selectors, language-specific configuration, and real-time preview.\\n3. The automatic selection of the appropriate template based on the visitor\\u0027s language.\\n4. The configuration steps for the EmailJS template and the admin interface.\\n5. The workflow example for an English-speaking visitor.\\n6. The advantages of the new approach, including simplicity, flexibility, and maintainability.\\n7. The testing instructions and the available variables in the admin templates and the EmailJS template.\\n8. The migration process from multiple EmailJS templates to the single template_bxltl6i.\",\"/src/data/themes.js\":\"This file defines a collection of predefined themes inspired by popular websites. It exports the following:\\n\\n`predefinedThemes`: An object containing 21 different theme configurations, each with its own set of colors, typography, and layout settings.\\n\\n`getThemesByCategory()`: A function that returns an object with the themes grouped by their respective categories.\\n\\n`getThemeById(themeId)`: A function that retrieves a specific theme by its ID.\\n\\n`themeCategories`: An array of available theme categories.\\n\\nThe file provides a comprehensive set of pre-designed themes that can be used in a web application, allowing developers to easily apply consistent and visually appealing styles inspired by popular online platforms.\",\"/server/server-simple.js\":\"This file is an Express.js server that sets up a RESTful API for managing language data using a SQLite database. It includes middleware for handling CORS and parsing JSON and URL-encoded request bodies, as well as routes for accessing language data and a health check endpoint. The file also includes error handling and logging functionality.\\n\\n`SQLiteDatabase`: A class that encapsulates the SQLite database connection and provides methods for interacting with the database.\\n\\n`languageRoutes`: A module that defines the routes for the language-related API endpoints.\\n\\n`app.use(\\u0027/api/languages\\u0027, languageRoutes)`: Mounts the language-related routes on the `/api/languages` path.\\n\\n`app.get(\\u0027/api/health\\u0027, ...)`: Defines a route that returns the health status of the server and the database connection.\\n\\n`app.use(\\u0027*\\u0027, ...)`: Defines a catch-all route that returns a 404 error for any unmatched routes.\\n\\n`app.use((error, req, res, next) \\u003d\\u003e {...})`: Defines an error-handling middleware function that logs and responds to server errors.\\n\\n`app.listen(PORT, ...)`: Starts the Express.js server and logs the available endpoints.\",\"/src/components/admin/EmailProviderSettings.js\":\"This file is a React component called `EmailProviderSettings` that allows users to configure their email provider settings. It provides a user interface for selecting an email provider, configuring the provider\\u0027s settings, testing the connection, and saving the configuration.\\n\\n`EmailProviderSettings`: A React component that manages the email provider settings for a website.\\n\\n`handleProviderSelect`: Selects an email provider and updates the component\\u0027s state accordingly.\\n\\n`handleConfigChange`: Updates the configuration for the selected email provider.\\n\\n`togglePasswordVisibility`: Toggles the visibility of password fields for the selected email provider.\\n\\n`handleTestConnection`: Tests the connection to the selected email provider and updates the component\\u0027s state with the test result.\\n\\n`handleSaveConfiguration`: Saves the configuration for the selected email provider.\\n\\n`renderProviderCard`: Renders a card for an email provider, including its icon, name, description, and pricing information.\\n\\n`renderConfigurationForm`: Renders the configuration form for the selected email provider, including fields for configuring the provider\\u0027s settings.\",\"/src/components/admin/ThemedSectionSelector.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `ThemedSectionSelector` that allows users to select and add themed or standard sections to a website. The component displays a modal with a list of themed and standard sections, and allows the user to add them to the website.\\n\\n`ThemedSectionSelector`: A React component that displays a modal with a list of themed and standard sections, and allows the user to add them to the website.\\n\\n`handleAddThemedSection`: A function that adds a themed section to the website when the user clicks the \\\"Add\\\" button for a themed section.\\n\\n`handleAddStandardSection`: A function that adds a standard section to the website when the user clicks the \\\"Add\\\" button for a standard section.\",\"/server/test-server.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it sets up a simple Express.js server with the following functionality:\\n\\n1. Imports the necessary modules (Express.js, CORS, and dotenv).\\n2. Configures the CORS middleware to allow cross-origin requests from the specified origin (or localhost:3000 by default).\\n3. Parses incoming JSON data.\\n4. Defines two simple API routes:\\n - `/api/health`: Returns a JSON response with the server status and the current timestamp.\\n - `/api/test`: Returns a JSON response with a simple message.\\n5. Starts the server and listens on the specified port (or 3002 by default).\\n\\nThe main purpose of this file is to set up a basic Express.js server with a few simple API endpoints for testing and health checking purposes.\",\"/src/components/RTLWrapper.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`RTLWrapper`: A React component that applies the right-to-left (RTL) direction to the document based on the current language, and adds or removes the \\u0027rtl\\u0027 class to the body element for special styles.\\n\\n`useEffect`: A React hook that is used to apply the RTL direction and language code to the document, and add or remove the \\u0027rtl\\u0027 class to the body element, whenever the current language changes.\\n\\nThis file is a React component that wraps other components and applies the appropriate RTL direction and language settings based on the current language context.\",\"/server/server-hybrid.js\":\"This file is an Express.js server that provides an API for managing languages. It includes the following functions:\\n\\n`initializeDatabases`: Initializes the database connection, first trying to connect to a MySQL database and then falling back to a SQLite database if the MySQL connection fails.\\n\\n`query`: A universal function for executing SQL queries, which abstracts the differences between MySQL and SQLite.\\n\\n`execute`: A universal function for executing SQL statements, which abstracts the differences between MySQL and SQLite.\\n\\n`startServer`: Starts the Express.js server and listens for incoming requests.\\n\\nThe file also includes several routes for interacting with the languages data, such as:\\n\\n- `GET /api/health`: Provides a health check for the server and the database connection.\\n- `GET /api/languages`: Retrieves all the languages.\\n- `GET /api/languages/active`: Retrieves the active languages.\\n- `GET /api/languages/default`: Retrieves the default language.\\n- `POST /api/languages`: Creates a new language.\\n- `PATCH /api/languages/:id/toggle`: Toggles the active/inactive state of a language.\\n- `PATCH /api/languages/:id/default`: Sets a language as the default.\\n\\nThe file also includes error handling and graceful shutdown functionality.\",\"/src/components/sections/ContactSection.js\":\"This file contains a React component called `ContactSection` that renders a contact section with a form and contact information. The component uses various hooks and services to handle the form submission, display contact information, and format office hours.\\n\\n`ContactSection`: A React component that renders a contact section with a form and contact information.\\n\\n`handleSubmit`: A function that handles the form submission and sends the contact email using the `unifiedEmailService`.\\n\\n`handleChange`: A function that updates the form data state when the user types in the form fields.\\n\\n`formatOfficeHours`: A function that formats the office hours information based on the site settings and translates the day labels.\\n\\n`getCurrentOpenStatus`: A function that checks the current open status of the office based on the office hours configuration.\",\"/GUIDE_TEMPLATES_EMAIL_MULTILINGUES.md\":\"This file is a guide for using a multilingual email template system. It provides an overview of the features, configuration instructions, API documentation, usage examples, and troubleshooting tips. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather serves as a comprehensive guide for the email template system.\",\"/src/components/LanguageDebug.js\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`LanguageDebug`: A React component that provides a debug interface for the language context, displaying information about the active languages, all languages, the default language, and the values stored in the browser\\u0027s local storage.\\n\\nThe file imports the `useLanguage` hook from the `LanguageContextDB` context and uses it to access the language-related functions and data. The component renders a fixed panel on the bottom right of the screen, displaying the language-related information and providing buttons to toggle the English and Spanish languages.\",\"/src/components/headers/AllThemedHeaders.js\":\"This file contains several React components that represent different header styles for a website or application. Each component is designed to provide a unique and visually appealing header layout with various navigation features. The file does not contain any classes, but it does include the following functions:\\n\\n`DefaultHeader`: A header with a fixed top bar, a floating navigation pill, and a mobile menu overlay.\\n\\n`GitHubHeader`: A header with a fixed top bar, a floating navigation pill, and a mobile menu overlay, similar to the `DefaultHeader`.\\n\\n`AirbnbHeader`: A header with a centered logo, a search bar-style navigation, and a mobile menu.\\n\\n`SpotifyHeader`: A header with a slim top bar and a side navigation indicator.\\n\\n`SlackHeader`: A header with a split layout, a colorful navigation pill, and a mobile menu.\\n\\n`NotionHeader`: A header with a clean design, animated underline tabs, and a mobile menu.\\n\\n`StripeHeader`: A header with a gradient top bar and floating navigation buttons.\\n\\n`FigmaHeader`: A header with a fixed top toolbar and a grid-based navigation module.\\n\\n`DiscordHeader`: A header with a dark gaming style and hexagonal navigation.\\n\\n`LinkedInHeader`: A header with a professional style and breadcrumb-style navigation.\\n\\n`ShopifyHeader`: A header with an e-commerce style and tabbed navigation.\\n\\n`NetflixHeader`: A header with a cinematic style and an overlay navigation.\\n\\n`InstagramHeader`: A header with an Instagram-style stories navigation.\\n\\n`WhatsAppHeader`: A header with a chat-style bubble navigation.\\n\\n`BehanceHeader`: A header with a portfolio-style masonry navigation.\\n\\n`UberHeader`: A header with a transport-style map-based navigation.\\n\\n`CursorHeader`: A header with a futuristic AI-style navigation.\\n\\n`TwitchHeader`: A header with a streaming-style chat-based navigation.\\n\\n`MediumHeader`: A header with a blog-style typewriter navigation.\"}"}
